name: Lisa's Fried Ice Cream
recipeIngredient: 8 balls ice cream (any flavor), about 1 quart
recipeInstructions:
1. The day before serving, prepare the batter by mixing flour, sugar, salt, vegetable oil, and egg yolks in a mixing bowl.
2. Gradually stir in the beer. Refrigerate overnight.
3. Coat each ice cream ball thoroughly with the crushed cornflakes. Place them in the coldest part of the freezer and freeze overnight.
4. Heat peanut oil to 375 Degrees F (190°C) in a deep fat fryer or deep pot using a thermometer.
5. Beat egg whites until stiff peaks form.
6. Gently fold the beaten egg whites into the batter.
7. Coat two or three ice cream balls completely and heavily with the batter.
8. Carefully lower the coated ice cream balls into the hot oil.
9. Maintain the oil temperature at 375 Degrees F (190°C) by adjusting the heat as necessary.
10. Fry the ice cream until golden brown, turning occasionally for even cooking.
11. Remove the fried ice cream using a slotted spoon and drain briefly on paper towels.
12. Serve immediately.
13. Repeat with the remaining ice cream balls.
